content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 ,J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Doug Karpa 
Legal Program Director 
Turtle Island Restoration Network 
PO Box 370 
Forest Knolls, CA 94933 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-15-161 
Dear Mr. Karpa: 
August 31, 2015 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding your obligations under the conflict 
of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 
Please note that the Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person 
whose duties under the Act arc in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 
83 l 14(b); Regulation 18329(b)( 1 ).) However, the Commission does not provide advice to a third 
party about a public official's duties under the Act. (Section 83114; regulation 18329.) Thus, we 
cannot respond to your specific questions about members of the Marin County Board of 
Supervisors and do not expressly discuss them herein. 
However, we can respond to your questions about your liability under Section 83116.5. 
Section 83116.5 provides: 
"Any person who violates any provision of this title, who purposely or 
negligently causes any other person to violate any provision of this title, or who 
aids and abets any other person in the violation of any provision of this title, shall 
be liable under the provisions of this chapter. However, this section shall apply 
only to persons who have filing or reporting obligations under this title, or who 
are compensated for services involving the planning, organizing, or directing any 
activity regulated or required by this title, and a violation of this section shall not 
constitute an additional violation under Chapter 11 (commencing with Section 
91000)." 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Go\'emment Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections I 8 I IO through I 8997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-15-161 
Page No. 2 
This section is only applicable to persons with filing duties under the Act. Since you do not 
have filing responsibilities under the Act, and based on your description of your conduct, you would 
not be subject to Section 83116.5. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
JWW:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
eneral Counsel 
eneral Counsel 
ion

sections.conclusion: This section is only applicable to persons with filing duties under the Act. Since you do not have filing responsibilities under the Act, and based on your description of your conduct, you would not be subject to Section 83116.5.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the requestor have liability under Political Reform Act Section 83116.5 for involvement in activities related to Marin County Board of Supervisors members' potential conflicts of interest?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The requestor is not subject to Section 83116.5 liability because they do not have filing responsibilities under the Political Reform Act and their described conduct does not meet the statute's applicability requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear question and answer content. FPPC explains limitation on providing advice about public officials while addressing requestor's specific liability question. Document dated August 31, 2015.

QUESTION: Does the requestor have liability under Political Reform Act Section 83116.5 for involvement in activities related to Marin County Board of Supervisors members' potential conflicts of interest?

CONCLUSION: This section is only applicable to persons with filing duties under the Act. Since you do not have filing responsibilities under the Act, and based on your description of your conduct, you would not be subject to Section 83116.5.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Doug Karpa's request regarding potential liability under Political Reform Act Section 83116.5, clarifying that the Commission cannot advise on public officials' duties but can address the requestor's personal liability, concluding that the requestor is not subject to Section 83116.5 due to lack of filing obligations.
